title: Taxonomy of NoSQL Databases and Big Data Storage Systems
description: A visual breakdown of the four main types of NoSQL databases, their core characteristics, and their placement within a big data ecosystem alongside processing frameworks.
relationships: Client Application accesses data from all four NoSQL Database types
All NoSQL Database types are built for horizontal scaling on a Distributed File System cluster
MapReduce/Spark Processing Framework analyzes data stored in the Distributed File System
Key-Value Stores use a simple key-to-value data model
Document Databases store data in JSON, BSON, or XML documents
Column-Family Stores organize data into columns and rows
Graph Databases focus on relationships between entities using nodes and edges
